account.changeDescription: Verify your current password, then change it to a new one.
account.setDescription: Set a password on this account to enable id/pw login.

install.issueDescription: Install the claude/codex wrapper (shim) so your usage is sent to toard. The token is tied to you, so usage is attributed to your account. Prompt and code content are not collected — only usage metadata such as token counts, model, cost, and computer name (host) is sent.
install.issueDescriptionWithContent: Install the claude/codex wrapper (shim) so your usage is sent to toard. The token is tied to you, so usage is attributed to your account. By default prompt and code content are not collected — only usage metadata is sent; you can turn on content collection below.
install.checkDescription: Verify that data is actually being received after installation.
install.hintWhich: • which claude → ~/.toard/bin/claude must resolve first (shim takes precedence).
install.hintUsage: • After that, using claude adds to My usage.
install.hintPrereq: • Prerequisite: the real Claude Code/Codex must be installed; we collect that CLI's telemetry.
install.devicesDescription: Even when you use the same account on multiple computers, usage is broken down per device. To stop sending the device name set TOARD_DISABLE_HOST=1; to show a different name set TOARD_HOST_LABEL=alias.
install.noDevices: No devices yet. After installing the shim, using claude/codex will make devices appear here.

onboarding.reissueConfirmDescription: The previous token is revoked immediately. Machines you already set up must replace the token in ~/.toard/credentials with the new value for collection to continue.
onboarding.issueSuccess: A new token has been issued — it has been filled into the install command below.
onboarding.newTokenNotice: New token — shown only now. Copy and run the command below.
onboarding.newTokenReissueHint: Reissuing revokes the previous token immediately.
onboarding.issueHint: Issue above and your token is filled into the command below.
onboarding.collectContentLabel: Also collect content <muted>(opt-in) — prompts and responses are stored encrypted; only you can view them in <link>My history</link>.</muted>
onboarding.collectContentGated: Content collection must be enabled by an administrator on the server.
onboarding.oneLinerDescription: Installs the binary + ~/.toard/credentials + PATH automatically. The endpoint {endpoint} is injected automatically. After installing, use claude/codex as usual and your usage is sent. To also collect Claude Desktop and IDE extension usage, it injects telemetry env into ~/.claude/settings.json (claude-env, applied from new sessions — restart any running app; to turn it off run toard-shim claude-env off).
onboarding.uninstallDescription: Reverts the shim · credentials · PATH setup · claude-env (settings.json) · codex [otel] block (a backup is left for each file). The real claude/codex is left untouched.

connection.noToken: No token issued — issue a token first.
connection.pollingHint: Run claude once in another terminal. Claude Desktop and IDE extensions are only picked up after you restart the app to start a new session. It shows up automatically when received (waits up to 2 minutes).
connection.timeoutHint: Nothing was received within 2 minutes. Check the shim install, PATH (which claude), and token setup, then try again. If you only use Claude Desktop or IDE extensions, run toard-shim claude-env on and then restart the app.

errors.issueTokenFailed: Failed to issue token. Please try again.

timezone.title: Display timezone
timezone.description: Timezone used for "today" boundaries and chart axes on usage screens. Auto follows your browser's timezone.

appearance.title: Preferences
appearance.description: Theme, color, and dashboard view apply to this device only; the display timezone is saved to your account.
